mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS toward the zero lower bound lower the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of 2008-11, down sharply from prior year
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS monthly change and 20-day forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX returns following rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers a short-horizon negative repricing of equities as analysts revise earnings expectations downward within five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2008-11, up from 6.1% two months prior
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase of recession
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE rises >0.3pp m/m, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in HOUST and RSAFS indicates a joint collapse of housing investment and consumer spending that compounds aggregate demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline year-over-year simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 791k units (near historic lows) and RSAFS weak as of late 2008
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-RSAFS decline indicator produces 60-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional distribution (t-test p>0.05), or single-series models achieve equal or higher Rank IC than the joint interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: During a regime of confirmed GDP contraction, declining INDPRO reflects deepening manufacturing recession that suppresses corporate earnings outlooks, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days; outside contraction regimes this relationship is expected to be weak or absent.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 contracting in Q3 2008 and INDPRO declining through Nov 2008
regime_hint: GDP contraction regime (two or more consecutive negative quarters)
failure_condition: During GDP contraction regimes, 20-day forward SPX returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly more negative than during expansion regimes (interaction coefficient p>0.10), rejecting regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation falls below 1%, signaling deflation risk, markets anticipate aggressive further monetary and fiscal policy support, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as policy-put expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.71 as of 2008-10, decelerating sharply amid falling energy prices
regime_hint: deflation-risk crisis regime with aggressive policy easing
failure_condition: When CPI YoY < 1% is triggered, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month declines in PAYEMS reflect ongoing labor market contraction that continuously erodes consumer income and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days on an always-on basis.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136,167k as of 2008-11, declining sharply for multiple consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative PAYEMS months do not coincide with below-median SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is contracting while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously being cut aggressively toward zero, markets price in strong forthcoming policy support that outweighs near-term growth deterioration, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon driven by the joint signal rather than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter while FEDFUNDS falls more than 1pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 contracting in 2008Q3 while FEDFUNDS cut from ~2% to 0.39% within months
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-contraction/FEDFUNDS-cut signal fails to produce 60-day forward returns significantly above the unconditional mean (p>0.05), or single-series FEDFUNDS models perform equally well, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month INDPRO decline exceeding 1% signals an acute acceleration of industrial contraction that triggers a sharp short-horizon negative equity reaction within five trading days as forward earnings guidance is revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173 as of 2008-11 following sharp sequential declines
regime_hint: acute industrial contraction phase of recession
failure_condition: Following INDPRO declines exceeding 1% m/m, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or effect is statistically insignificant (p>0.10) across the sample.
